behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_FISH::look' (examine) event for a Fish item: when a player/process inspects the fish, the script constructs the literal message "fish " and asks the Fish item to 'bark' (display/announce) that message to the inspecting actor.

The handler performs minimal housekeeping: allocate/build the message, push the inspecting pid and message, call Item::bark with the Fish instance, free the temporary message, propagate the bark return value into the process result, then suspend/return.
No global flags or persistent state are read or written by this handler in the observed excerpt; behavior is entirely local and UI-facing.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 0 (U8P_FISH::look) -> allocate/prepare literal string "fish " -> push viewer pid and string -> call external Item::bark -> free temporary string -> push/return bark result -> implied/pop sequence -> suspend/return.
The flow is linear with a single external call and lightweight cleanup; there are no branches or state mutations observed.
open_questions: Is the value returned by Item::bark used by higher-level logic, or merely propagated for engine bookkeeping? (appears to be propagated but exact use is unknown)
Exact calling convention and ordering of pushed parameters (which stack slot holds the viewer pid vs the message pointer) is inferred from calls but not explicitly documented.
Whether the 'bark' action can trigger additional side effects (dialogs, sound, or logging) beyond displaying the string is unobserved here and left to Item::bark's implementation.

main_function.name: U8P_FISH::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player's 'U8P_FISH::look' (examine) action on a Fish item by producing the short description string "fish " and invoking the item's display/announce routine so the inspecting player sees the word.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_FISH::look) — triggered when an actor/player looks at or examines the Fish item

function_map.name: U8P_FISH::look
function_map.role: Event handler for U8P_FISH::look/examine on the Fish item; composes and emits a short description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to display or announce a short text message from an item/process to a target process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Cause the Fish item to display/announce the provided string ("fish ") to the inspecting player/process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_FISH::look' (examine) event for a Fish item: when a player/process inspects the fish, the script constructs the literal message "fish " and asks the Fish item to 'bark' (display/announce) that message to the inspecting actor.
